key_insight: EquiFiLM adds charge-conditioned feature-wise modulation to an existing equivariant force field so it can learn external-state effects, like charging, without breaking E(3)-equivariance.
engineering_relevance: This enables foundation ML force fields to model driven processes such as charge injection and photoexcitation with only a small amount of new DFT data and no inference-cost penalty.
method: Insert a per-layer FiLM block into scalar channels of an equivariant MLFF backbone.
Condition the potential energy surface on external variables such as charge while preserving exact equivariance.
Demonstrate the approach on liquid water with MACE-MatPES, producing the E-MACE model.
evidence_signal: On four training charges, E-MACE reduced force RMSE from 21.3 to 6.96 meV/Å and energy RMSE from 6.1 to 0.1 meV/atom, and it remained accurate and stable across seven held-out charges.
watch_item: Performance depends on having representative conditioning data across the external-state range; strong extrapolation beyond the tested charges may still be risky.
paper_title: EquiFiLM: Charge-Conditioned Equivariant Force Fields via Feature-wise Linear Modulation
